title: Feed UI cleanup: edge-to-edge insets, typography weights, AuthorLine layout
description:
## Why

Visual diff between the production FeedScreen and the design-system reference (openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/screens/) surfaces four cleanup items. None are net-new features; all are bugs in shipped composables.

## Bugs

### 1. Edge-to-edge insets — content drawing under system bars

`MainActivity` calls `enableEdgeToEdge()` ✓. Manifest has `windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` ✓. But content layers don't consume insets:

- `FeedScreen.kt:160-185` Scaffold's `padding` lambda is consumed only by `InitialLoading`'s LazyColumn (line 168). Empty / InitialError / Loaded variants ignore it — visible in the screenshot: status bar overlays the first card.
- `LoadedFeedContent` LazyColumn has no `contentPadding` for Scaffold padding propagation.
- `PullToRefreshBox` indicator draws at the absolute top — under the status bar.
- `FeedEmptyState` / `FeedErrorState` are `fillMaxSize` with no inset awareness.
- `LoginScreen.kt:77-90` has no Scaffold and no `imePadding()` / `safeDrawingPadding()` despite an `OutlinedTextField` — IME would obscure the field on smaller screens.

Goal: maintain edge-to-edge (system bars stay translucent / scrim-free for the killer-feature look) AND give every interactive surface proper safe-area padding.

### 2. Typography rendering all weights as bold

`designsystem/.../Fonts.kt:40-43`:

    val RobotoFlexFontFamily =
        FontFamily(
            Font(resId = R.font.roboto_flex),
        )

Roboto Flex is a **variable** font with a `wght` axis. The single Font entry has no `FontVariation.Settings`, so every `FontWeight.Normal/SemiBold/Bold` call from `Type.kt` maps to the same physical asset rendered at its default wght axis (~700-ish). Result: body text looks bold, headlines look extra-bold, the type scale collapses.

Fix: declare a Font per FontWeight in the FontFamily, each with `variationSettings = FontVariation.Settings(FontVariation.weight(N))`. Honors the variable wght axis.

### 3. AuthorLine row wraps timestamp on long handles

`PostCard.kt:142-160` AuthorLine renders displayName + composite-handle-and-timestamp as two Texts. The composite Text via `R.string.postcard_handle_and_timestamp` ("@%1\$s · %2\$s") has no max-lines or weight, so a long handle pushes the timestamp onto a second visual line.

Reference (`openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/components/ThreadPost.kt:94-105`) shows the canonical fix: three separate Texts, `Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))` between handle and timestamp, handle gets `maxLines = 1, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis`.

Splits the `postcard_handle_and_timestamp` string resource into `postcard_handle` and `postcard_relative_time_format`.

### 4. State composables ignore Scaffold padding

`FeedEmptyState`, `FeedErrorState` use `fillMaxSize().padding(...)` only — Scaffold's `innerPadding` from `FeedScreen` is dropped on the floor when the parent dispatches to them.

## Out of scope (separate tracking)

- Self-thread connector line — new capability, separate bd task (sibling under nubecita-m28).
- TopAppBar / bottom nav / FAB and their inset handling — owned by nubecita-cif (App shell epic).
- Status-bar translucent scrim — owned by app shell once the TopAppBar lands.
- Link card embed render (currently shows "Unsupported embed: link card") — owned by nubecita-aku.
- Quoted-post embed render (currently shows "Unsupported embed: quoted post") — owned by nubecita-6vq.

## Process

Drive via openspec change `add-feed-ui-cleanup`. Spec deltas on `feature-feed` (insets) + `design-system` (typography weights, AuthorLine).

## Acceptance

- Cold start of FeedScreen on a 120Hz device: no content draws under the status bar; pull-to-refresh indicator appears below the status bar; last visible card has bottom padding clear of the gesture bar.
- LoginScreen: tapping the handle field opens IME without obscuring the input.
- FeedScreen body text renders at FontWeight.Normal visually distinct from the SemiBold displayName; visual hierarchy of titleMedium / bodyLarge / bodySmall is restored.
- A post with a 30+ char handle (e.g. `someverylonghandle.bsky.social`) still renders displayName + truncated handle + timestamp on ONE line with timestamp pinned right.
- Empty + InitialError full-screen states draw clear of system bars.
- All existing screenshot tests updated; new baselines committed.
close_reason: Merged in PR #54. Edge-to-edge insets propagated across FeedScreen branches; RobotoFlexFontFamily declares per-weight Fonts with FontVariation.Settings; bundled roboto_flex.ttf replaced with the official 1.6 MB variable .ttf (the prior file was a 304 KB corrupted blob starting with newline bytes); AuthorLine simplified to weight(1f) handle + right-pinned timestamp; bodyLarge.lineHeight tightened from 26.sp to 22.sp for feed density. Openspec change add-feed-ui-cleanup archived in same commit.

title: Upstream fix: DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized must handle nonce + token refresh in one 401 cycle
description:
## Background

Confirmed by nubecita-09o: cold start after access token TTL produces FeedError.Unknown ("Something went wrong"). Repro logs show the bug lives in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider, not in nubecita.

## Bug shape (in atproto-kotlin oauth/.../DpopAuthProvider.kt:67-72)

After idle, the persisted OAuthSession has BOTH an expired access token AND a stale pdsNonce. The first XRPC GET produces a 401 with error=use_dpop_nonce + new dpop-nonce header. onUnauthorized short-circuits on the nonce branch:

    if (newNonce != null && newNonce != pdsNonce) {
        pdsNonce = newNonce; persistNonces(); return true
    }
    return refreshMutex.withLock { refreshTokens() }

The retry uses the new nonce + same expired access token → second 401 with error=invalid_token, exp claim failed. XrpcClient.query only retries once → XrpcError\$Unknown thrown.

User tapping Try again works only because the now-cached pdsNonce means the next 401 has no new nonce to update → onUnauthorized falls through to refreshTokens.

## Scope

Track work to land the upstream fix in atproto-kotlin AND adopt it in nubecita. Out of scope: workarounds in nubecita (e.g. proactive refresh in DefaultXrpcClientProvider) — per user_owns_atproto_kotlin memory, library changes are in scope.

## Tasks

- [ ] File GH issue at kikin81/atproto-kotlin with the trace + proposed fix.
- [ ] Implement upstream fix. Leading candidate: in onUnauthorized, do BOTH recoveries in a single call — update nonce AND inspect the JWT exp claim (or just attempt refresh after nonce update if a fresh nonce was returned alongside an expired-token-shaped 401). Returning true means "I did everything I could; retry once."
- [ ] Cut a new atproto-kotlin release.
- [ ] Bump atproto version in nubecita (gradle/libs.versions.toml).
- [ ] Smoke test: kill app, wait > 1h, cold start. Logcat should show POST <token endpoint> between the 401 and the eventual 200 — no FeedError.Unknown.

## Acceptance

- Cold start after token TTL loads the timeline on the first attempt — no "Something went wrong" screen.
- adb logcat -s AtProtoHttp shows: 401 use_dpop_nonce → POST token endpoint (200) → retry GET (200), all without user interaction.

## Refs

- Diagnostic logs PR: kikin81/nubecita#50 (closes nubecita-09o)
notes: Library bump (PR #51 / commit 66600e1) merged 2026-04-26. atproto = 5.1.2. Issue stays open until nubecita-09o (diagnostic-logs PR #50) merges, because the on-device smoke-test trace in the acceptance criteria requires the AtProtoHttp / FeedRepository log scaffolding to verify the fix end-to-end.
close_reason: Upstream fix shipped: kikin81/atproto-kotlin#34 → atproto 5.1.2; nubecita bumped via PR #51 (commit 66600e1). Diagnostic-logs scaffolding for end-to-end verification merged via PR #50 (commit d05c0bb). On-device smoke test (cold start after >1h idle: GET 401 use_dpop_nonce → POST token endpoint 200 → GET 200, no FeedError.Unknown) is owner-side and not blocking issue closure.

title: Introduce build-logic composite with convention plugins
description:
Extract the repeated Android build-script boilerplate into a composite-build convention-plugin setup (NiA pattern). Creates build-logic/convention/ as an includeBuild sibling with five convention plugins:

- nubecita.android.library — com.android.library, compileSdk/minSdk, JVM 17, consumer-rules.pro, sort-dependencies
- nubecita.android.library.compose — library + kotlin.plugin.compose + Compose BOM + base Compose deps
- nubecita.android.hilt — dagger.hilt.android + ksp + hilt-android runtime + compiler
- nubecita.android.feature — library + compose + hilt + :core:common + :designsystem
- nubecita.android.application — com.android.application + targetSdk + Hilt+Compose for :app

Modules declare alias(libs.plugins.nubecita.android.*) and get all the boilerplate for free. Pure refactor — zero behavior change; ./gradlew build output should match before/after.

Scope: create build-logic composite + five plugins + plugin aliases in libs.versions.toml + migrate :app, :designsystem, :core:auth to use them. Nothing else. Unblocks the feature-login PR on clean foundations.
close_reason: Merged in PR #31. Convention plugins + module migration landed.

title: Scaffold :core:auth Gradle module
description:
Create a new :core:auth Android library Gradle module that will house auth-related code (session storage, AtOAuth binding, AuthRepository, deep-link utilities). Mirrors the :designsystem scaffold pattern (nubecita-7dp). Establishes the :core:* convention for non-UI cross-cutting libraries.

Scope:
- New module at core/auth/ with com.android.library plugin
- namespace = net.kikin.nubecita.core.auth
- Empty package with placeholder; real impl lands with nubecita-nss
- settings.gradle.kts include + :app implementation(project(':core:auth'))

Acceptance: ./gradlew :core:auth:assembleDebug succeeds; :app depends on :core:auth.
close_reason: Merged in PR #28 (commit 1d2abd6); openspec change archived as 2026-04-24-add-core-auth-session-storage.

title: Hilt bindings for AtOAuth and authenticated XrpcClient
description: Extend the Hilt graph with authenticated AT Protocol bindings: provide AtOAuth (constructed with the hosted client metadata URL from nubecita-e16, the encrypted OAuthSessionStore from nubecita-nss, and the existing Ktor HttpClient) so feature code can @Inject AtOAuth to drive beginLogin/completeLogin/createClient. After a successful login, authenticated calls use AtOAuth.createClient() which returns an XrpcClient backed by a DpopAuthProvider. Key constraint: the clientMetadataUrl must be read from BuildConfig or a string resource, not hard-coded, so dev (GitHub Pages) and prod (custom domain) builds can diverge without editing the module. Design context in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-atproto-hilt-module-design.md.
close_reason: Merged in PR #32. :feature:login api/impl + :core:auth AtOAuth bindings + screenshot tests landed.

title: Establish bd-driven branch/commit workflow with helper scripts
description: Add a 'Workflow' section to CLAUDE.md documenting the convention (Conventional-Commit-prefixed branches, bd id embedded, Refs/Closes footers) and ship two shell helpers (scripts/bd-start, scripts/bd-finish) that automate the ceremony so Claude and humans both stay consistent. Unblocks clean execution of every downstream bd task.
design: Branch prefixes mirror Conventional Commit types 1:1 (feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs/test/perf/ci/build/style). Type inferred from bd type: task->chore, feature->feat, bug->fix. Slug = kebab-cased title, capped ~50 chars. Footer uses 'Refs: <id>' on WIP commits and 'Closes: <id>' on the PR body.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md has a Workflow section; scripts/bd-start and scripts/bd-finish exist, are executable, and handle the golden path (claim+branch, push+PR).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Copilot review findings on PR #13 (beads hooks + AGENTS.md)
description: Address 6 Copilot comments on https://github.com/kikin81/nubecita/pull/13: (1) replace hard-coded INSTALL_PYTHON paths in .beads/hooks/pre-commit and commit-msg with portable detection, (2) reorder beads integration block before exec so it actually runs, (3) deduplicate prepare-commit-msg vs scripts/git-hooks/prepare-commit-msg, (4) trim AGENTS.md to remove manual quick-ref that duplicates the BEADS INTEGRATION block.
close_reason: Applied 4 fixes: portable pre-commit/commit-msg hooks, beads block reordered before exec, prepare-commit-msg delegates to scripts/git-hooks/ canonical, AGENTS.md deduped. Commit 3376dd6, pushed to origin/f/beads.

title: Host OAuth client metadata JSON
description:
Host the AT Protocol OAuth client-metadata.json that Bluesky's authorization server fetches during PAR. Two-step approach: (1) Development: serve from GitHub Pages at https://kikin81.github.io/nubecita/oauth/client-metadata.json via docs/oauth/client-metadata.json in this repo (mirrors the atproto-kotlin sample). (2) Production: swap to a custom domain when launch-ready.

Per atproto-oauth skill: client_id must equal the hosting URL exactly; redirect_uris use single-slash (net.kikin.nubecita:/oauth-redirect — matches the app's applicationId, not the 'com.nubecita.app' placeholder originally sketched on this ticket); application_type=native; dpop_bound_access_tokens=true; token_endpoint_auth_method=none; Content-Type: application/json.

Note: the dev→prod URL swap is a one-way door that invalidates all existing sessions (client_id change). Fine while pre-launch, flag in release notes. GitHub Pages must be enabled for the repo (source: deploy from docs/ on main, or Actions workflow) — currently not configured, this task includes enabling it.
acceptance_criteria: GET https://<host>/nubecita/client-metadata.json returns valid JSON that passes atproto OAuth client validation.
close_reason: Merged in PR #30 (commit 5a3c707). GitHub Pages enabled via 'gh api POST repos/kikin81/nubecita/pages' with source main/docs. Client metadata will be live at https://kikin81.github.io/nubecita/oauth/client-metadata.json once Pages completes its first build.

title: Implement AT Protocol OAuth flow
description: Full AT Protocol OAuth: PAR, DPoP, authorization code exchange, refresh. Use atproto-auth helpers where available. Custom Tab for authorization. Redirect back via app link.
design: Client metadata JSON must be publicly hosted (see sibling task). Redirect URI registered as Android App Link with verified digital asset link.
acceptance_criteria: User can complete full OAuth against bsky.social; refresh works; tokens stored via secure-storage task; deep link handler resumes flow after redirect.
close_reason: Merged in PR #33. Custom Tab launch + redirect callback + Navigator (in :core:common) + AuthRepository.completeLogin all landed. Post-login routing decisions deferred to nubecita-30c.

title: Secure session token storage
description: Persist access/refresh JWTs across process death using EncryptedSharedPreferences (or DataStore + Tink). Survive uninstall-protection not required. Cleared on signOut.
acceptance_criteria: Tokens survive process kill; cleared on signOut; never readable from plaintext preferences on a rooted inspection.
close_reason: Merged in PR #28 (commit 1d2abd6); openspec change archived as 2026-04-24-add-core-auth-session-storage.

title: AuthRepository with app-password implementation
description: Interface AuthRepository { signIn, signOut, currentSession: Flow<Session?>, refresh() }. Default impl calls com.atproto.server.createSession via atproto-auth. Returns typed domain Session, not raw lexicon output.
acceptance_criteria: Repository is Hilt-injectable; signIn returns Result<Session>; currentSession emits null after signOut.
close_reason: Superseded — app-password auth dropped in favor of OAuth-only (see nubecita-ck0). Decision made on 2026-04-24.

title: Login screen UI with OAuth MVI flow
description: LoginContract (State/Event/Effect) + LoginViewModel extending MviViewModel, driving the atproto-oauth flow. State: handle input, isLoading, errorMessage. Events: HandleChanged, SubmitLogin, OAuthRedirectReceived, ClearError. Effects: LaunchCustomTab(authorizationUrl), NavigateToFeed, ShowError. On SubmitLogin: resolves handle, calls AtOAuth.beginLogin, emits LaunchCustomTab effect. MainActivity forwards the deep-link redirect back to the ViewModel (via nubecita-ck0's callback plumbing) which calls AtOAuth.completeLogin, then emits NavigateToFeed on success. UI uses :designsystem components — single handle TextField, primary button, inline error display. No app-password field (dropped on 2026-04-24 — nubecita-ym3 superseded by nubecita-ck0).
acceptance_criteria: User can enter a Bluesky handle, submit, see a loading state, and be redirected to the authorization server via a Chrome Custom Tab. On successful redirect back through the deep-link callback, the app navigates forward to the feed; on failure, an inline error is displayed without leaving the login screen. No app-password input anywhere on the screen.
close_reason: Merged in PR #32. The dependency on nubecita-ck0 is for the eventual Custom Tab launch + redirect callback wiring, which is ck0's scope. uf5's own scope (UI + MVI + screenshot baselines) is complete; the LaunchCustomTab effect is the documented handoff point.

title: Implement Material 3 Expressive theme with Material You dynamic color
description: Define NubecitaTheme composable inside :designsystem with Material 3 Expressive color schemes (light/dark/high-contrast), expressive typography, shape tokens reflecting the 'little cloud' aesthetic (soft corner radii), and Material You dynamic color opt-in on Android 12+ via dynamicLightColorScheme/dynamicDarkColorScheme. Expose MotionTokens for the subtle scale/fade signature.
design: Use androidx.compose.material3 1.3+ expressive APIs. Fallback palette when dynamic color unavailable. Tokens in Color.kt, Type.kt, Shape.kt, Motion.kt.
acceptance_criteria: NubecitaTheme wraps MainActivity content; sample preview renders light + dark + dynamic; typography/shape tokens consumed by at least one reusable component.
close_reason: Merged in PR #27 (commit bcb9e0e)

title: Scaffold :designsystem Gradle module
description: Create a new :designsystem module under ./designsystem/ with its own build.gradle.kts. Depends on compose-bom, material3, material3-adaptive. No app-layer dependencies. Register in settings.gradle.kts and have :app depend on it.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :designsystem:assembleDebug succeeds; :app depends on :designsystem.
close_reason: Merged in PR #27 (commit bcb9e0e)

title: Hilt module providing atproto client + auth session
description: Create a Hilt @Module (@InstallIn(SingletonComponent)) that constructs the atproto runtime client with the auth session from atproto-auth. Expose @Provides bindings for the high-level API classes used by feature code (e.g. BskyApi, AuthClient).
acceptance_criteria: App graph resolves; a throwaway test @Inject's the client and successfully calls resolveHandle for bsky.app.
close_reason: Merged via PR #23 (squash-commit 3ddf953). OpenSpec change archived to openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-23-add-atproto-hilt-module/; atproto-networking capability created in openspec/specs/.

title: Add atproto-kotlin 5.0.0 dependencies (models, runtime, oauth)
description: Add io.github.kikin81.atproto:models:5.0.0, :runtime:5.0.0, :oauth:5.0.0 to the version catalog (gradle/libs.versions.toml) and app/build.gradle.kts. Verify Maven Central resolves without credentials.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :app:dependencies shows all three artifacts; build succeeds.
notes: Published coordinates differ from the epic's initial shorthand — artifacts live under group io.github.kikin81.atproto as at-protocol-{models,runtime,oauth} (note: oauth, not auth). Per maintainer 2026-04-21, upstream rename to io.github.kikin81:atproto-* is planned; rewire this integration once the renamed release lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create MviViewModel base class and marker interfaces
description: Port the slabsnap pattern into app/src/main/java/.../ui/mvi/: UiState, UiEvent, UiEffect marker interfaces plus abstract MviViewModel<S,E,F> that exposes uiState StateFlow, buffered effects Channel, abstract handleEvent(E), protected setState{reducer} and sendEffect(F).
acceptance_criteria: Files compile; base class is abstract and generic; effects use BUFFERED Channel; one sample sealed Contract.kt is added under a feature package to prove the shape.
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Authentication (OAuth)
description: Implement AT Protocol OAuth for Nubecita. App-password path was dropped on 2026-04-24 (see nubecita-ym3 closed as superseded by nubecita-ck0). Scope: OAuth flow (PAR, DPoP, authorization code exchange, refresh via atproto-oauth's AtOAuth), secure session storage, Hilt bindings for AtOAuth + authenticated XrpcClient, hosting OAuth client metadata JSON, OAuth login screen UI with deep-link callback handling, and sign-out.
design: AuthRepository abstraction hides app-password vs OAuth. Secure storage via EncryptedSharedPreferences or DataStore + Tink. OAuth client metadata JSON served from a static host (GitHub Pages or similar).
acceptance_criteria: User can log in with handle + app password; session persists across process death; sign-out clears credentials; OAuth path documented with clear follow-up tasks.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: Design system package
description: Stand up a :designsystem Gradle module with Material 3 Expressive theming (color, typography, shape, motion), Material You dynamic color support, and reusable composables (Post, Avatar, Buttons, etc.) used across feature screens. This is the single source of visual truth for Nubecita's 'little cloud' aesthetic.
design: Separate Gradle module :designsystem. Depends only on compose-bom + material3. Feature modules depend on :designsystem, never redefine theme tokens.
acceptance_criteria: :designsystem module compiles; NubecitaTheme wraps the app; dynamic color toggles on Android 12+; at least one reusable component (e.g. PostCard) is consumed by a feature screen.

title: Epic: atproto-kotlin SDK integration
description: Wire io.github.kikin81:atproto-4.9.0 (models, runtime, auth) from Maven Central into the app. Provide Hilt-bound client instances so feature code can call lexicons like app.bsky.feed.getTimeline without touching construction details.
acceptance_criteria: Gradle sync pulls models/runtime/auth artifacts; Hilt module exposes the atproto client; a smoke-test call (e.g. resolveHandle) succeeds from a throwaway test.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: MVI architecture foundation
description: Establish a reusable MVI skeleton that every ViewModel in the app must conform to. Based on the slabsnap pattern: generic MviViewModel<S:UiState, E:UiEvent, F:UiEffect> with StateFlow-backed uiState, buffered Channel for one-shot effects, abstract handleEvent, protected setState{reducer} and sendEffect helpers. Lives in a shared UI layer so feature modules depend on a single abstraction.
design: Generic abstract base class. Marker interfaces (UiState/UiEvent/UiEffect). Per-screen sealed Contract.kt file with State data class + Event/Effect sealed interfaces. Inspired by ~/code/slabsnap/app/src/main/java/us/kikinsoft/slabsnap/ui/mvi/.
acceptance_criteria: MviViewModel base class compiles and is unit-testable; one sample screen (e.g. login) extends it; doc comment explains contract pattern.

title: PostCard self-thread connector line (drawWithContent at avatar gutter)
description:
## Why

Bluesky shows a vertical 2dp connector through the avatar gutter to visually link consecutive posts in a self-thread (same author, replies in a chain). Reference design at openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/components/ThreadPost.kt:65-87 shows the canonical pattern:

    Modifier.drawWithContent {
        drawContent()
        val x = 41.dp.toPx()  // 20 gutter + 22 avatar half - 1 line half
        if (connectAbove) drawLine(connectorColor, Offset(x, 0f), Offset(x, avatarTop), 2.dp.toPx())
        if (connectBelow) drawLine(connectorColor, Offset(x, avatarBottom), Offset(x, size.height), 2.dp.toPx())
    }

Plus a `ThreadFold` composable (ThreadPost.kt:174-232) for "View full thread" elision between non-adjacent siblings.

## Scope

Two halves:

### A. Render side (small)

- Add `connectAbove: Boolean = false`, `connectBelow: Boolean = false` parameters to `PostCard`.
- Implement the connector via `drawWithContent` at avatar gutter x=41dp, color = `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outlineVariant`, 2dp stroke.
- Add `ThreadFold` composable in `:designsystem` matching the reference.
- Screenshot tests for: connectAbove only, connectBelow only, connectAbove + connectBelow, ThreadFold.

### B. Detection side (larger, may need its own task)

- Determine which posts in a feed page belong to the same self-thread (author chain via `reply.parent.author.did == post.author.did`).
- Set the `connectAbove/connectBelow` flags on PostUi at the mapper layer or at the FeedViewModel projection layer.
- Decide: collapse non-adjacent siblings into ThreadFold, or render every post inline?
- Bluesky web defaults to inline + ThreadFold for >2 hops.

## Dependencies

- Render side (A) can land first as a no-op if the FeedViewModel always passes `false` for both flags. Detection side (B) wires the flags to real values.

## Out of scope

- Quoted-post (record embed) rendering — nubecita-6vq.
- Reply-to-other-user thread (the parent post by a different author) — Bluesky shows this with a different visual treatment, separate ticket.
- Post detail screen thread rendering — separate epic once we have a detail screen.

## Acceptance

- A `@Preview`-ed feed with three consecutive posts by the same author renders one continuous vertical connector through their avatar gutters.
- The middle post's connector touches the avatar borders top + bottom; the first post connects only below; the last post connects only above.
- ThreadFold renders three vertical dots + "View full thread · N more" between non-adjacent siblings.
- Existing PostCard tests/screenshots unchanged when both flags default to false.

title: Tap-to-play with single-player coordinator
description:
## Scope

Wire the actual ExoPlayer playback. The hard part is the coordinator: at most one player instance is materialized at a time, attached to the most-visible video card; scrolling away pauses + releases the binding (player can be reused for the next visible card).

## Design (to be confirmed by nubecita-sbc.2 proposal)

- `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator` (singleton, scoped to FeedScreen lifecycle): owns one `ExoPlayer` instance.
- `LoadedFeedContent` observes `LazyListState.layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo`, identifies the topmost video card whose visible-fraction exceeds some threshold (e.g. 0.6), and asks the coordinator to attach the player to that card's `PlayerView`.
- Tap on a video card: emits a `FeedEvent.OnVideoPlay(post)` (or similar) routed through the VM. VM updates `currentlyPlayingPostId` in state. The coordinator binds when a card is in-view AND its post id matches `currentlyPlayingPostId`; binds nothing otherwise.
- Scrolling the playing card off-screen: coordinator pauses + releases the binding; state retains `currentlyPlayingPostId` so scrolling back resumes from the last position.
- Screen exit: `DisposableEffect` releases the ExoPlayer entirely.

## Tasks

- New `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator` in `:feature:feed:impl` (or a `:core:media` module if nubecita-sbc.1 created one).
- Extend `FeedState` with `currentlyPlayingPostId: String?` (or whatever the design lands on).
- Extend `FeedEvent` with `OnVideoPlay(post)` / `OnVideoPause`.
- Wire the coordinator into `FeedScreen` via `LaunchedEffect(listState) { snapshotFlow { ... }.collect { coord.bindMostVisibleVideo(...) } }`.
- Custom controls overlay: play/pause + mute toggle + (optional) fullscreen affordance.
- Memory + battery test: scroll a feed of 20 videos for 2 minutes, confirm only one decoder active in `adb shell dumpsys media.player`, confirm no player leaks across screen exit.

## Acceptance

- Playing one video and scrolling away pauses it; scrolling another video into view does NOT autoplay (tap-to-play is preserved); only one ExoPlayer instance exists at any time.
- 120Hz scrolling target unchanged with the coordinator active.
- All FeedScreen unit + screenshot tests still pass.
- Manual: feed with 5 video posts plays correctly without ANR or jank.

## Depends on

- nubecita-sbc.1 (deps + smoke test) and nubecita-sbc.3 (thumbnail render) merged first. Strongly recommended: nubecita-sbc.2 (proposal) lands first to lock the design decisions.
notes:
Phase C implementation complete (per openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`, tasks 4.1-4.7, 5.x except 5.6 archive). Manual smoke (4.8) is the developer's responsibility on a real 120Hz device once this PR merges.

## Files

- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/PlaybackHint.kt` — `enum { None, FocusLost }`. Coordinator-internal hint for the bound card.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/VideoBindingTarget.kt` — pure `mostVisibleVideoTarget(layoutInfo, postsById)` function + `VideoBindingTarget(postId, playlistUrl)` data class. Visibility math extracted from FeedScreen so it can be unit-tested in isolation.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator.kt` — owns one `ExoPlayer`, scope-cancellable `Mutex`-serialized state mutations, audio-focus contract per spec. Production construction via `createFeedVideoPlayerCoordinator(context, audioManager)` factory; primary constructor takes injected `ExoPlayer + DefaultTrackSelector` so unit tests use relaxed mocks for the player and exercise the audio-focus contract on a mocked `AudioManager` without standing up the real Android framework. Includes `internal` test hooks `notifyBecomingNoisy()` and `handleSystemFocusChange(focusChange)` so tests fire system events without reflecting into AudioFocusRequest (the `getOnAudioFocusChangeListener()` accessor isn't exposed on unit-test Android stubs).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbed.kt` — phase-C overload `PostCardVideoEmbed(video, postId, coordinator)` that dispatches to a private `PostCardVideoEmbedAutoplay` at runtime and falls back to the phase-B static-poster overload under `LocalInspectionMode.current`. Renders `PlayerSurface` + mute icon + "tap to resume" overlay when bound. Mute icon uses `Icons.AutoMirrored.Outlined.VolumeUp/VolumeOff` (the legacy non-mirrored variants are deprecated). New strings `postcard_video_mute / unmute / resume`.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedScreen.kt` — `LoadedFeedContent` constructs the coordinator via `remember { createFeedVideoPlayerCoordinator(...) }` (skipped under `LocalInspectionMode.current` for layoutlib safety), wires `DisposableEffect(Unit) { onDispose { coordinator.release() } }`, supplies `videoEmbedSlot = { video -> if (coordinator != null) PostCardVideoEmbed(video, postId, coordinator) else PostCardVideoEmbed(video) }`, and runs the scroll-gated bind flow `combine(snapshotFlow { isScrollInProgress }, snapshotFlow { mostVisibleVideoTarget(...) })`.
- `feature/feed/impl/build.gradle.kts` — `testOptions.unitTests.isReturnDefaultValues = true` (otherwise `AudioAttributes.Builder.setUsage` etc. throw "not mocked" against the Android stub jar at coordinator construction time).

## Tests

- `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinatorTest` — 9 audio-focus-contract tests covering all 5 spec scenarios + a few additional edge cases (denied focus request stays muted, release without unmuting doesn't abandon focus, resume after focus loss reacquires + clears hint). Uses `mockkConstructor` to intercept `AudioAttributes.Builder` + `AudioFocusRequest.Builder` chains so the production path constructs successfully against the Android stub jar.
- `MostVisibleVideoTargetTest` — 6 tests for the visibility math (topmost video wins, non-video skipped, threshold edge, unknown keys, empty, viewport clipping).

## Verification

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:bundleRelease` — green
- `./gradlew :app:lintRelease` — green
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` — green repo-wide (added 15 tests; modified 0)
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest :designsystem:validateDebugScreenshotTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green
- `pre-commit run --all-files` — green
- `openspec validate add-feature-feed-video-embeds --strict` — green

## Remaining manual work (task 4.8)

On-device smoke matrix that requires a 120Hz physical device — NOT performable from CI / unit tests. The spec lists 7 scenarios:
1. Music-not-hijacked (Spotify keeps playing while scrolling video posts; pauses on explicit unmute).
2. Single-player invariant via `adb shell dumpsys media.player`.
3. 120Hz scrolling with no jank or decoder errors.
4. Incoming call while unmuted → "tap to resume" affordance.
5. Headphones unplug while unmuted → "tap to resume" affordance.
6. Screen exit → 0 active players in `dumpsys media.player`.
7. Rotation → autoplay restarts muted from position 0.

Plan: run the manual smoke after merge using a real device. Document any findings in this issue's notes; file follow-ups for any divergence.

title: PostCard video embed: thumbnail-only render (no inline playback)
description:
## Scope

Land the video data path through the mapper + PostCard without yet wiring an ExoPlayer. The card shows the static poster (thumbnail), a duration badge, and a centered play affordance. Tapping does nothing yet — the next subtask wires that.

## Why thumbnail-first

Two reasons: (1) it proves the FeedViewPostMapper + EmbedUi extension for `app.bsky.embed.video#view` independently of player wiring (smaller PR, easier review), and (2) it's the only thing that ships if for any reason we revert the player work — feeds with videos still look correct, they just don't play.

## Tasks

- Extend `data/models/EmbedUi` with `data class Video(...)` carrying: poster URL (thumbnail), HLS URL, aspect ratio, duration in seconds, alt text.
- Extend `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedViewPostMapper.kt`: dispatch `app.bsky.embed.video#view` → `EmbedUi.Video(...)`. Pure function + fixture JSON test.
- Extend `designsystem/.../PostCard.kt` (or a sibling `PostCardVideoEmbed.kt`): when `embed is EmbedUi.Video`, render `NubecitaAsyncImage(posterUrl, aspectRatio)` with a centered Material3 outlined "play_arrow" overlay + a duration chip in the bottom-right corner. Tap opens nothing for now (TODO: wire in next subtask).
- Strings: `R.string.post_video_play_label` etc. for accessibility.
- Screenshot tests for the new variant (light/dark, with + without alt text overlay).

## Acceptance

- A feed item with `app.bsky.embed.video` renders the poster card, duration chip, and play affordance.
- Tap is a no-op (placeholder lambda); no crash.
- Mapper unit tests cover (a) well-formed video view, (b) missing thumbnail (poster falls back to gradient placeholder), (c) malformed view returns Unsupported.
- Screenshot baselines committed.

## Out of scope

- Any ExoPlayer integration.
- Tap-to-play.
- Single-player coordinator.

These land in nubecita-sbc.4.
notes:
Phase B implementation complete (per openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`, tasks 3.1-3.7).

## Spec amendment (mid-flight)

Phase A's lexicon check (in nubecita-sbc.1's notes) found that `app.bsky.embed.video#view` does NOT carry a duration field. The original openspec proposal/design assumed otherwise. This phase amends the openspec deltas accordingly:

- `EmbedUi.Video.durationSeconds: Int?` (was `Int`) — mapper passes `null` in v1; render layer gates the duration chip on non-null.
- `EmbedUi.Video.posterUrl: String?` (was `String`) — render layer falls back to a gradient placeholder when null.
- `EmbedUi.Video.aspectRatio: Float` stays non-null — mapper supplies a 16:9 fallback (`1.777f`) when the lexicon omits it. The render layer needs a stable measurement before the poster loads.

Spec deltas now have explicit scenarios for each branch (well-formed, missing thumbnail, missing aspect ratio, empty playlist).

## Files

- `data/models/.../EmbedUi.kt` — added `Video` variant with the nullable fields above.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../data/FeedViewPostMapper.kt` — `VideoView` arm now calls `toVideoEmbedUi()` which falls through to `Unsupported` when playlist is blank.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../data/FeedViewPostMapperTest.kt` — replaced the "video → Unsupported" assertion with three new tests (well-formed, no-thumbnail, no-aspectRatio, empty-playlist falls through).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbed.kt` — new file. Phase B variant: poster + optional duration chip only. Aspect ratio applied to outer container BEFORE poster loads. Inline `formatDuration` helper for `m:ss` / `h:mm:ss` (no string resource — digit-only, locale-insensitive).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedScreen.kt` — supplies `videoEmbedSlot = { video -> PostCardVideoEmbed(video) }` to PostCard.
- `designsystem/.../PostCard.kt` — added `videoEmbedSlot` parameter (default no-op); `EmbedSlot` dispatches `EmbedUi.Video` to it. Updated previews — replaced the "unsupported (video)" preview with "unsupported (record-with-media)" and added a video-embed preview using a slot stub.
- `feature/feed/impl/src/screenshotTest/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbedScreenshotTest.kt` — 4 variants × 2 themes = 8 baselines (with/without poster, short/long duration). Validated.

## Lexicon-duration follow-up

The `formatDuration` code path is dead in v1 (mapper always passes null). It's kept ready for a future phase that sources duration:
1. Bluesky lexicon evolution adds `duration` to `app.bsky.embed.video#view` — mapper trivially populates `durationSeconds`.
2. Phase C (or later) parses the HLS manifest's `EXT-X-PLAYLIST-TYPE: VOD` segment durations after the player loads.

Either way, no spec or composable changes needed beyond the mapper.

## Verification

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:lintRelease` — green (5 warnings, all baseline-filtered)
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` — green repo-wide (added 3 mapper tests; modified 1)
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest :designsystem:validateDebugScreenshotTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green
- `pre-commit run --all-files` — green (includes commitlint + openspec validate --strict)

title: Openspec proposal: video playback strategy in the feed
description:
## Scope

Run the openspec-propose flow for a `add-feature-feed-video-embeds` (or similarly-named) change. Capture the design decisions before any production code lands.

## Decisions to lock down in the proposal

1. **Player lifecycle / instance count.** Single shared `ExoPlayer` attached to the most-visible video card (LazyColumn `visibleItemsInfo` driven), released on screen exit. Or N players (one per card on screen, capped at K). Lean: single player + coordinator. (Cite the bsky-app iOS / Android pattern in the proposal.)
2. **Default playback mode.** Tap-to-play (poster + play button until user taps) vs autoplay-muted-on-Wi-Fi. Lean: tap-to-play for v1 — simpler, no autoplay-policy gating, matches Mastodon/Twitter behavior; revisit autoplay in a v2 once metrics show engagement gap.
3. **Audio focus + mute defaults.** When playing, claim transient audio focus; default unmuted (since user explicitly tapped); mute toggle in the player controls overlay.
4. **HLS adaptive vs fixed.** Use `media3-exoplayer-hls` adaptive selector; in the feed start at the lowest variant and let ABR upgrade. PostDetail screen (separate epic) starts at the user's connection-class default.
5. **Controls surface.** Custom minimal controls (play/pause, mute, fullscreen) over `media3-ui`'s default chrome — chrome looks like a YouTube embed, not a feed-native item.
6. **Error states.** Network failure mid-playback → fall back to thumbnail + retry tap; entire stream missing → render the same poster card without play affordance.
7. **Performance budget.** No more than one decoder active at a time; cards more than N positions away from viewport release their `MediaItem` references; thumbnail bitmap caching coordinated with Coil.

## Process

- Use the `openspec-propose` skill (or `/opsx:propose`).
- The openspec change should produce a spec delta on `feature-feed` adding ~3-4 new requirements about player coordination + render contract.
- Don't archive until the implementation subtasks (thumbnail render + tap-to-play coordinator) merge.

## Acceptance

- Openspec change directory exists at `openspec/changes/add-feature-feed-video-embeds/` (or chosen name) with proposal.md + design.md + tasks.md + specs/feature-feed/spec.md delta.
- `openspec validate <name> --strict` passes.
- Implementation subtasks updated to reference the change name in their notes.
close_reason: openspec proposal landed in PR #56; spec amendments in sbc.3 PR #58

title: Add Media3 / ExoPlayer dependency + smoke-test HLS playback
description:
## Scope

Add Media3 to the dependency catalog and wire the minimum surface area needed to play an HLS stream in a debug Android build. No PostCard integration here — just prove the runtime path works.

## Tasks

- Add to gradle/libs.versions.toml: `media3 = "<latest stable>"`, plus `media3-exoplayer = ...`, `media3-exoplayer-hls = ...`, `media3-ui-compose = ...` (NOT the legacy `media3-ui` artifact — we're using `PlayerSurface`, not `PlayerView`, per Decision 3 in the openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`).
- Decide module: probably a new `:core:media` Android library (mirrors :core:auth split) so feature modules depend on it via the `nubecita.android.library` convention plugin. Alternative: ship the dep directly from `:feature:feed:impl` and skip the new module — defer until the proposal (sibling subtask) lands.
- Smoke test: a one-off debug `@Composable` that constructs a single `ExoPlayer`, sets a Bluesky HLS URL (e.g. `https://video.bsky.app/...m3u8`), and renders video through the Compose-native `PlayerSurface` from `media3-ui-compose` (file-level `androidx.annotation.OptIn(UnstableApi::class)`). Confirms HLS variant negotiation, Tink + DataStore-DB don't conflict, ProGuard rules in release variant.
- Document version + module placement decision in the bd notes for the parent epic.

## Acceptance

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` succeeds with the new deps.
- HLS smoke composable plays a 480p variant of a sample bsky video on a 120Hz device without dropped frames at app idle.
- ProGuard rules (if needed for ExoPlayer) added to consumer-rules.pro.

## Non-goals

- PostCard integration (separate subtask).
- Single-player coordinator (separate subtask).
notes:
Phase A complete (per openspec change add-feature-feed-video-embeds, tasks 2.1-2.4).

## Decisions
- **Media3 version**: 1.10.0 (matches openspec change pin).
- **Module placement (Decision 6)**: inline in `:feature:feed:impl`. Single v1 consumer (FeedScreen video coordinator + PostCardVideoEmbed both land in `:feature:feed:impl`); `:core:media` can be promoted later if/when PostDetail epic actually needs to share. Avoids pre-emptive module split.
- **Catalog entries** added to `gradle/libs.versions.toml`: `media3-exoplayer`, `media3-exoplayer-hls`, `media3-ui-compose` (NOT the legacy `media3-ui`; using PlayerSurface, not PlayerView).
- **Smoke composable**: `feature/feed/impl/src/main/kotlin/.../ui/FeedVideoSmokeTest.kt` — private `@Preview` exercising the Media3 + media3-ui-compose path. Uses `androidx.annotation.OptIn(UnstableApi::class)` (NOT `kotlin.OptIn`) since Media3's `UnstableApi` is a Java annotation gated by Lint's UnsafeOptInUsageError, not Kotlin's `@RequiresOptIn` mechanism — `kotlin.OptIn` produces a "no effect" warning. **Replaced/deleted in phase C** (sbc.4) by the real coordinator + PostCardVideoEmbed.

## APK / bundle size delta
Baseline (main, no Media3) vs HEAD (Media3 deps + smoke composable). All builds via `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug :app:bundleRelease`.
- Release `.aab`: 18,106,049 → 20,312,814 bytes (+2,206,765 bytes, +2.1 MB).
- Debug `.apk`: 79,417,083 → 86,047,706 bytes (+6,630,623 bytes, +6.5 MB).

The release delta is ~5x the openspec proposal's ~400 KB estimate. Reason: the proposal's estimate assumes R8 minification, but `:app`'s release build type has `isMinifyEnabled = false` (per `AndroidApplicationConventionPlugin.kt`). With minification enabled later, R8 will tree-shake unreferenced Media3 classes and the delta should drop closer to the proposal's prediction. Phase C should re-measure after wiring the real consumer, and we can revisit minification as a separate optimization issue if the unminified delta becomes a concern.

## Lexicon check (task 1.2)
`app.bsky.embed.video#view` is exposed by atproto-kotlin 5.1.2 as `io.github.kikin81.atproto.app.bsky.embed.VideoView` with fields: `alt`, `aspectRatio`, `cid`, `playlist`, `presentation`, `thumbnail`. **No deserializer gap; no upstream issue filed.**

⚠ **Finding for phase B (sbc.3)**: the `view` lexicon does NOT carry a `duration` field (verified at `kikinlex/generator/lexicons/app/bsky/embed/video.json`). The openspec change's proposal/design assumes a "duration in milliseconds" field that doesn't exist in the upstream Bluesky lexicon. Phase B's `EmbedUi.Video` design must either drop `durationSeconds` (no duration chip rendered) or source duration from somewhere else (HLS playlist EXT-X-TARGETDURATION at runtime, after the player loads the manifest). Recommend phase B brainstorm reconciles this — likely path is "no duration chip in v1; revisit if/when Bluesky adds the lexicon field". Tracked here so phase B doesn't re-litigate.

## Task 1.3 (sample HLS URL on-device verification)
Deferred — that's a manual on-device step that the developer (you) does after the deps land. Not blocking the phase A PR. Logging here: when running on a 120Hz device, swap the placeholder URL in `FeedVideoSmokeTest` for a real `https://video.bsky.app/...m3u8` URL pulled from a public bsky video post, then wire the composable into a temporary debug screen (or call it from `MainActivity` under a debug flag) and confirm the HLS variant negotiates + audio is silent + `dumpsys media.player` shows exactly 1 active player.

## Build verification
- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:bundleRelease` — green
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:testDebugUnitTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green
